intro: Westia angolensis, commonly known as Westian or Angolan Westia, is a plant species belonging to the family Westiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Westia angolensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Westia angolensis is typically used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional African medicine, where it is valued for its ability to reduce inflammation and fight infections.
